records.ety: [OE. schar, AS. scear; akin to OHG. scaro, G. schar, pflugshar, and E. shear, v. See Shear.]

defs: The part (usually an iron or steel plate) of a plow which cuts the ground at the bottom of a furrow; a plowshare.
The part which opens the ground for the reception of the seed, in a machine for sowing seed.

records.ety: [OE. share, AS. scearu, scaru, fr. sceran to shear, cut. See Shear, v.]

defs: A certain quantity; a portion; a part; a division; as, a small share of prudence.
Especially, the part allotted or belonging to one, of any property or interest owned by a number; a portion among others; an apportioned lot; an allotment; a dividend.
Hence, one of a certain number of equal portions into which any property or invested capital is divided; as, a ship owned in ten shares.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shared (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sharing.]

defs: To part among two or more; to distribute in portions; to divide.
quotes: Suppose I share my fortune equally between my children and a stranger.

defs: To partake of, use, or experience, with others; to have a portion of; to take and possess in common; as, to share a shelter with another.
quotes: While avarice and rapine share the land.

defs: To cut; to shear; to cleave; to divide.
quotes: The shared visage hangs on equal sides.

defs: To have part; to receive a portion; to partake, enjoy, or suffer with others.
quotes: A right of inheritance gave every one a title to share in the goods of his father.
